name.description: Required if no event_id is provided.
Required if no event_id is provided.

layoutSpec.description: You can alternatively supply a 'custom_setup' string from your team_settings, along with an 'attendees' integer.

wizard.description: One of 'wizard' or 'layoutSpec' must be provided.

layoutSpec.description: One of 'wizard' or 'layoutSpec' must be provided.

rotation.description: The rotation relative to the viewport.

ignore_doors_and_columns.description: True and undefined result in skipping collision checks with permanentObjects

best_effort_capacity.description: Whether a failure to seat all attendees is acceptable. False will throw a fatal error if not all attendees can be seated. True will seat the max possible and add a ctx error message

errors_in_diagram.description: Add error text to the diagram.

nudgeOnCollide.description: On collision, nudge tables slightly to a nearby valid position if any exists. Note: This may slightly compromise a setup's natural shape.

updatedRects.description: Uses an improved algorithm to detect bounding boxes in a floorplan. It usually results in larger valid areas being detected.

floorElementsBetween.description: Number of floor elements that should be placed in a row or column before an aisle is placed.

size.description: For round tables, radius is required. All other tables require length and width.

cullAdditionalTables.description: Whether to cull extraneous tables beyond the requested attendee count, if any. It has no bearing on whether tables that collide with existing objects should be culled.

placeEvenlyAcrossLayout.description: Should the automator attempt to place tables evenly across the space? False will limit tables to the largest 'region of interest' within the room.

removeChairs.description: The number of chairs to remove for crescent tables. Ignored for other floor elements.

rotateCrescentOffset.description: The offset for where the missing chairs should be for crescent tables. Ignored for other floor elements.

startTime.description: The date/time (UTC) at which the event begins. Must be in ISO 8601 format. Value must not be later than `endTime`.

endTime.description: The date/time (UTC) at which the event ends. Must be in ISO 8601 format. Value must not be earlier than `startTime`.

public.description: Whether the event is publicly accessible.

usesMetric.description: Whether the event's diagram uses metric units.

externalEventId.description: An (optional) external identifier for the event, which can be used to associate it with an event in an external system. An error will occur if this value is already in use by another event.

roomId.description: An identifier for the room in which the event is to occur.

externalDiagramId.description: An (optional) external identifier for the diagram, which can be used to associate it with a booking in an external system. An error will occur if this value is already in use by another diagram.

eventUrl.description: A URL that can be used to view the event in the web application.

public.description: Whether the event is publicly accessible.

usesMetric.description: Whether the event's diagram uses metric units.

createdAt.description: The date/time at which the event was created.

updatedAt.description: The date/time at which the event was last updated.

diagramUrl.description: A URL that can be used to view the diagram in the web application.

pdfUrl.description: A URL that can be used to view/download the diagram as a PDF document.

startTime.description: The date/time (UTC) at which the event begins. Must be in ISO 8601 format. Value must not be later than `endTime`.

endTime.description: The date/time (UTC) at which the event ends. Must be in ISO 8601 format. Value must not be earlier than `startTime`.

public.description: Whether the event is publicly accessible.

usesMetric.description: Whether the event's diagram uses metric units.

externalEventId.description: An (optional) external identifier for the event, which can be used to associate it with an event in an external system. An error will occur if this value is already in use by another event.

externalPropertyId.description: An external identifier for the property at which the event is to occur.

externalRoomId.description: An external identifier for the room in which the event is to occur.

externalDiagramId.description: An external identifier for the diagram, which can be used to associate it with a booking in an external system. An error will occur if this value is already in use by another diagram.

title.description: A short, human-readable summary of the problem.

status.description: The HTTP status code corresponding to this problem.

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.

key.description: A canonical name representing the type of the error.

requestId.description: A unique identifier for this request, which can be used to help with debugging and support.

example.title: Validation Failed
example.detail: One or more input fields failed validation

message.description: A human-readable explanation of why the field failed validation.

field.description: The path to the field that failed validation. In the case of nested fields, this will be a dot-separated path, plus an array index for fields nested in an array (e.g., 'diagrams[0].roomId' would indicate a validation failure in the 'roomId' field of the first provided value in the 'diagrams' field).

value.description: The original value that was provided for the field. May be of any type.

diagramUrl.description: A URL that can be used to view the diagram in the web application.

eventUrl.description: A URL that can be used to view the event containing the conflicting diagram in the web application.

conflictingDiagrams.description: An array of objects representing the existing diagrams that have the provided external diagram ID. This property will be present only when the response's `key` value is `external_diagram_id_conflict`.

eventUrl.description: A URL that can be used to view the conflicting event in the web application.

conflictingEvents.description: An array of objects representing the existing event(s) that have the provided external diagram ID. This property will be present only when the response's `key` value is `external_event_id_conflict`.

ErrorResponse_ExternalIdConflict-layout-.description: An error response that indicates that a provided external event or diagram ID is already in use by an existing event or diagram.

example.title: External Diagram ID already in use
example.detail: One or more of the provided external diagram IDs are already in use

get.summary: get a room by ID

post.summary: Accepts data about external events and persists them
post.description:
This api creates a diagram in a specified room.
Unlike most of our other apis, this uses externally provided property and room ids instead of our ids, and requires coordination with our professional services team to map the rooms.
booking_id must be unique for each event and function_id must be unique for each diagram.
If you are interested in using this api, please contact your account manager or Cvent sales.
